label: ESC J command unit
evidence: LQ-500 command table/manual notes list ESC J n as n/180 inch. Service manual Figure 2-47 says paper advances 1/180 inch per phase switch.
next_step: For ESC J, expect n command units to correspond to n paper-feed phase switches unless trace proves intermediate scaling.

label: PB mask 04h paper-feed drive/hold control
evidence: Figure 2-47 says paper-feed signal PB2 is active low: low turns Q27 on and supplies +24 V; not driven supplies +5 V through R36/D11 for hold. Firmware 549C clears PB bit mask 04h and 5498 sets PB bit mask 04h. In the VV62!=0 branch, record value 01h reaches 549Ch and drives PB2 low; other values reach 5498h and return PB2 high/hold.
next_step: The final post-motion release is the VV37=20h state at 07D0h/07D6h: 540Dh selects copied EF60=00 from the 708Eh timing record, so the PB mask 04h branch takes 5498h and returns PB2 high/hold.

label: PB mask 18h paper-feed phase outputs
evidence: Figure 2-47 identifies PB3 as phase A/B and PB4 as phase C/D. Firmware 096A maps VV16 & 18h directly onto PB & 18h, matching PB3/PB4 if service-manual bit numbering is zero-based. The 18h value is a bit mask for two PB port bits, not a pin name.
next_step: Trace 0908/093E/0953/095F calls from the FE1 path and count PB mask 18h state changes per EE7A/EF40 unit.

label: PB bits 3/4 2-2 phase excitation sequence
evidence: Service manual excitation table maps PB3/PB4 states HH, HL, LL, LH to phase pairs A+C, A+D, B+D, B+C, and labels that table order clockwise/paper-forward while counterclockwise feeds paper in reverse. Firmware output states are PB & 18h values 18h, 08h, 00h, 10h. With reset VV16=CCh, PB & 18h starts at 08h; rotate-right 0953 steps 08h->00h->10h->18h->08h, matching increasing manual step order, and rotate-left 095F reverses it.
next_step: ESC J positive feed reaches EF61/VV61 bit0 clear and calls 0953h; ESC j reverse feed reaches EF61/VV61 bit0 set and calls 095Fh. This matches the manual's forward/reverse phase-order statement if PB3/PB4 bit numbering is zero-based.

label: FE1 paper phase/update order
evidence: The FE1 ISR checks VV37 mask 71h at 0675h before the phase path. State 1 therefore first routes through 07BBh, sets a drive-on state through 540Dh, jumps back to 067Ah, and only then calls 093Eh. For command feed, VV62=1 at 56B9/56BB, so each counted feed step reaches the PB bits 3/4 phase-update path before the relevant counter is decremented.
next_step: Use this ordering when comparing scope captures: PB2 drive should go low before the first PB3/PB4 phase change.

label: vertical distance representation
evidence: ESC J injects n directly as HL=00nn, while LF loads EF8B and passes it through 50EB before entering 2048h/256Eh. Service manual confirms the physical phase switch unit is 1/180 inch.
next_step: Resolve whether 2534h flag state changes scaling, or whether later EE7A/EE86 handling converts both forms to phase-switch counts.

label: esc_J_immediate_forward_feed
evidence: ESC table target for ESC J. Reads one byte through CALT 0080, sets H=00h, copies A to L, and falls into 2534h.
next_step: Use as the positive immediate-feed root.

label: esc_j_immediate_reverse_feed_fx80_compat
evidence: ESC table target for ESC j. Reads one byte through CALT 0080, sets H=80h, copies A to L, and jumps back to 2534h.
next_step: Use as the reverse-feed compatibility root.

label: shared_immediate_feed_or_advance_entry
evidence: Sets VVC1 bits E0h. In the immediate-feed path this reaches 1FEAh, then 2048h, then 256Eh with HL holding the signed distance.
next_step: Confirm the uncommon 2540h side path by tracing callers/flags.

label: shared_vertical_advance_dispatch
evidence: Common LF/FF/ESC J dispatch. Calls 105Ah, either diverts to render path 299Dh or prepares EE44/EE50/EE4E state and jumps to 256Eh.
next_step: Separate render-required versus pure-feed flag combinations.

label: setup_signed_vertical_advance_state
evidence: Stores signed feed/render distance in EE7A, records direction/style flags in VV38/VV39, and stores normalized absolute distance at EE86 after H bit 7 handling.
next_step: Map all EE7A/EE86 consumers and identify the exact unit scale.

label: update_page_position_after_advance
evidence: Updates vertical/page offset state from EF42/EF44/EF3E/EF47 and stores the result in EF11.
next_step: Correlate EF11/EF91 with documented top-of-form and page offset behavior.

label: process_pending_vertical_advance_distance
evidence: If EE7A has a nonzero magnitude, stores the distance in EF40, sets VV38 bit 08h, and calls 5676h after scheduler-availability checks.
next_step: This is the main bridge from command feed distance into timed output scheduling.

label: paper movement counter variables
evidence: The service manual says one phase switch is one 1/180-inch paper step. ESC J writes n as HL=00nn, 2864h stores the nonzero pending magnitude in EF40, and the 5676h scheduler copies that through the EF75 path into EF64 while setting VV62=1/VV63=0. FE1 calls 093Eh once for each counted feed step. Short moves use EF64=n; long moves use 5 lead steps plus EF64=n-10 plus 5 tail steps, so both paths produce n PB3/PB4 phase changes.
next_step: After the counted movement and tail delay, VV37=20h releases PB2 to high/hold through EF60=00 and 5498h before final interrupt masking.

label: schedule_output_from_ef38_state
evidence: Copies EF38..EF45 to EF6D..EF7A, writes F004=20h, then branches on VV6D.3. ESC J/ESC j set VV38.3 before calling 5676h, so the copied VV6D.3 is set and the scheduler takes 569Ah-56C5h, which derives EF61..EF64 from EF75 and jumps to 5582h.
next_step: Map the EF75 source fields back to EF40/EF42/EF44 so the remaining distance partition is explicit.

label: arm_timed_mechanism_record
evidence: Sets VV37=1, calls 55B1h to load timing data, calls 540Dh for initial output state, arms ETM1 from EF49, and enables FE1.
next_step: Tie ETM1 interrupt progression back to 0668h and the output-state updates.

label: load_mechanism_timing_record_into_ef49
evidence: Selects/copies a timing/control record from 7005h/7088h, with VV62/VV63 selecting the base and record index. For the ESC J path, 56B9/56BB sets VV62=1 and 56BCh sets VV63=0, selecting the first 7088h-family record. 55BA stores the record head in VV4D, then BLOCK with C=17h copies 24 bytes into EF49..EF60.
next_step: Decode the 708Eh record fields against the 0668h ISR state transitions.

label: normal timing record family
evidence: For the 7005h family, byte 0 is count 05h, byte 1 is index 04h, so the first 18h-byte record starts at 700Fh. 55BA stores the record head byte in VV4D; 55C6/55CB copy 24 bytes into EF49..EF60 because uPD7810 BLOCK copies C+1 bytes.
next_step: Decode the EF49 field meanings from the 0668h ISR and identify which VV63 record is selected by ESC J.

label: scheduler timing-record selection
evidence: The scheduler has two traced record-selection paths. With VV6D.3 set, 569Ah-56C5h writes EF61..EF64 from EF75, including VV62=1 and VV63=0, then enters 5582h. With VV6D.3 clear, 56D7h clears VV62, selects EF70 or EF73 as EF64, stores VV63 from VV72/VV7C, and jumps with HL=7005h to 558Dh. ESC J/ESC j use the VV6D.3-set path because 2864h sets VV38.3 before 5676h copies it.
next_step: Trace EF75 production and the later EF64 reload path to separate total move length from per-segment countdown.

label: paper-feed short-move threshold and timing seed
evidence: Service manual gives acceleration intervals 3.33, 2.87, 2.65, 2.53, then 2.50 ms steady; decel is reverse. The command-feed setup checks VV62==1 at 55D4h, loads 708Eh, forces H=00h, and uses DLT EA,HL against threshold 0x000B. With uPD7810 carry-skip semantics, counts below 0x0B skip the JR at 55E0h, set VV36 mask 04h, load EF51 from 725Fh, and jump to 5621h without rewriting EF64. Thus command counts 1..10 take this special short-move path and EF64 remains n.
next_step: Short moves do not walk the lead/tail lists; they count down EF64 directly through 06A6h after the initial 07BBh drive-on state.

label: paper-feed long-move steady segment count
evidence: For command-feed counts >=0x0B, 55E0h jumps to 55EEh. With the 708Eh record fields, VV4C=05h and VV54=05h are subtracted from EA; their high-bit flags are clear, so no halving is applied, and VV53 bit 7 is clear, so the result is not doubled. The normal long-move EF64 reload is therefore current_count-10 before the state machine continues. EF4F starts at 725Fh and EF57 starts at 7269h; the FE1 ISR walks these pointers at 0772h and 0799h.
next_step: The exact phase count is 5 lead steps through VV37=2/VV4C/EF4F, then n-10 middle steps through VV37=4/EF64, then 5 tail steps through VV37=8/VV54/EF57.

label: paper-feed command count boundaries
evidence: Count 0 does not schedule paper motion: the pending-distance bridge at 2864h only stores EF40 and calls 5676h for a nonzero magnitude. Counts 1 and 10 take the short path with EF64=1 or EF64=10. Count 11 is the first long-path count: EF64 becomes 1, bracketed by 5 lead and 5 tail steps, for 11 total phase changes.
next_step: Use n=1, n=10, and n=11 as scope-test cases if checking hardware behavior.

label: paper-feed lead/tail timing lists
evidence: The 708Eh record copies EF4F=725Fh and EF57=7269h. The FE1 ISR reads a word through EF4F at 0772h and advances that pointer; separately it reads a word through EF57 at 0799h and advances that pointer. ROM words at 725Fh begin 1086h, 0DC6h, 0CB8h, 0C24h, 0C00h; words at 7269h begin 0C24h, 0C24h, 0CB8h, 0DC6h, 0FFCh. Under the 0C00h=2.50 ms scale, 1086h is about 3.44 ms.
next_step: Treat the leading 1086h as an apparent ROM-revised first movement interval; verify whether reverse/path variants use the manual-nominal 0FFCh first interval elsewhere.

label: carriage home-seek short/long split
evidence: The 5253h carriage home-seek timed routine has a separate literal 10-step-shaped split: 529Ah walks up to nine table-driven intervals, 52BD subtracts 000Ah from the requested count, and 52C3 sends zero remainder to the decel/tail path instead of the long steady loop. The normal paper command-feed threshold is the 55D8h/708Eh gate.
next_step: Carry this only as a cross-reference; details belong in data/lq500_3c_carriage_path.tsv.

label: mechanism_output_state_dispatch
evidence: Dispatches VV37/VV62 state to either the 7007h PA/PB jump table or the simple PB mask 04h output. With uPD7810 skip semantics, VV62==0 reaches the 7007h jump table at 545Eh, while VV62!=0 reaches the PB mask 04h branch at 5495h. ESC J sets VV62=1, so its drive/hold windows use PB mask 04h.
next_step: The VV62==0 jump-table branch belongs with carriage/head timed output; paper command feed uses the VV62!=0 PB mask 04h branch and PB bits 3/4 phase updates.

label: PB mask 04h table-controlled drive window
evidence: 540Dh maps VV37 bits to an EFxx state byte. In the VV62!=0 PB mask 04h branch, value 01h takes the NEI-not-skipped path to 549Ch and clears PB mask 04h low, turning on +24 V drive. Other values skip the JR and execute 5498h, setting PB mask 04h high for hold. The 708Eh record gives value 01h for the active command-feed states EF4D/EF53/EF55/EF5B. The post-tail VV37=20h state falls through the selector to EF60; the 708Eh timing record copies EF60=00, so 540Dh calls 5498h for hold.
next_step: This closes the command-feed PB2 drive window: drive low before the first phase, low through counted phases and VV37=10h/EF59 settle, then high at VV37=20h/07D6h before final FE1 masking.

label: PB2 post-motion release to hold
evidence: After the tail list/counter finishes, 078Ah-0790h sets VV37=10h and calls 540Dh; state 10h selects EF5B=01, so PB2 remains low during the EF59 delay loaded at 0793h. The next FE1 pass sees VV37 bit 10h at 0699h, enters 07D0h, changes the state to VV37=20h, and calls 540Dh at 07D6h. For normal non-80h states, 540Dh maps bits 04h/02h/08h/01h/10h to EF53/EF4D/EF55/EF4B/EF5B; state 20h matches none and selects EF60. The selected 708Eh record copies EF60=00, so a zero record value takes the 5498h path, setting PB & 04h high for +5 V hold.
next_step: After this release, 07E1h schedules the EF5C delay, 0804h schedules EF5E, and 0836h masks FE1/clears motion flags without another PB mask 04h write.

label: startup_carriage_home_seek_entry
evidence: Startup-only path branches on raw PA mask 20h and calls 5253h with short/long carriage seek distances. It is not statically connected to ESC J/j.
next_step: See data/lq500_3c_carriage_path.tsv for the carriage trace and active HOME polarity caveat.

label: startup_carriage_home_seek_timed_sequence
evidence: Walks carriage timing tables around 7287h/72AFh, samples PA5 through PA mask 20h at 5306h, pulses PC7/TM through 0908h, and selects carriage current states through 546Ah/547Eh.
next_step: See data/lq500_3c_carriage_path.tsv for the carriage trace.
